event_blocks.summary: The Microsoft Build title screen, featuring an upbeat, futuristic synth track, transitions to the Microsoft Bing interface where a user selects 'More Creative' and requests an inspiring video script about AI and developers. The AI responds, 'Sure, I can do that for you,' initiating an animated text sequence. The AI-generated text, 'We’re entering a new era of AI. Where you will be able to build as fast as your imagination,' overlays a young superhero girl running, a Formula 1 car racing, and a sports crowd, prompting a female voice to exclaim, 'It's like we're in my mind. It's magical.' The text continues, 'Where your creativity will have no constraints,' over office collaborators working, as a male voice notes, 'We're innovating at a scale like never before,' and another adds, 'It takes what you prompted with and just runs with it.' A male voice then declares, 'Everything is linked, everything is easy to use,' and another emphasizes, 'We're accelerating how fast we can deliver solutions to our fans, to our business.' Finally, the text 'And you can focus on the big picture,' appears over a doctor and patient shaking hands, with a female voice explaining how it reduces provider computer time. The visuals remain vibrant and fast-paced, reinforcing AI's empowering role for developers and creators, all set to the continuous synth music.

It's magical,' and male voices reinforcing themes of innovation, ease of use, and accelerated delivery.

Doctor and Patient.description: A male doctor in a white lab coat shaking hands with a seated male patient in a clinical setting, symbolizing trust and human connection, with a female voice explaining how AI 'takes the hands-on time that the provider has to spend with the computer and puts that...' implying AI's role in freeing up professionals for more human-centric tasks.

event_blocks.summary: The video opens with a woman in an office discussing patient care, transitioning to a man and woman interacting, with a voiceover emphasizing amplified human capability. An inspiring text overlay appears, followed by scenes of a farmer and a village girl in rural India, highlighting opportunities for personal growth. A woman in a black blazer speaks passionately in a conference room, and a collaborative workspace group interacts with a robot, accompanied by typing sounds. Two women high-five, and a text message exchange is shown before the 'Microsoft Build' title screen appears with orchestral music. Satya Nadella, Chairman and CEO of Microsoft, confidently walks onto a stage at the Microsoft Build event, greeting an enthusiastic audience and discussing platform shifts and the 'Pursuit of the Dream Machine' timeline, referencing historical computing milestones like Vannevar Bush and Licklider, and the 'Mother of All Demos.' He highlights ChatGPT as a significant upgrade, comparing it to a 'steam engine' and a 'Mosaic moment for this generation of the AI platform,' emphasizing the profound changes across the software stack. Nadella then introduces Microsoft's developer tools: Codespaces for rapid environment setup, Dev Box for faster development environments, Copilot for enhanced productivity, and GitHub Actions and Azure Deployment Environments for streamlined workflows, all while emphasizing the desire to stay within VS Code and the command line. He discusses the pervasive integration of AI across Microsoft platforms, including Copilot in Viva, Dynamics 365, security, Bing, Edge, and LinkedIn, powered by Azure OpenAI APIs. Nadella announces over 50 new updates, including bringing Bing search grounding to ChatGPT, and then introduces Windows Copilot, designed to make every user a Windows power user. The Windows Copilot interface is demonstrated, showing AI-powered assistance for system adjustments, focus timers, dark theme activation, document summarization, and music recommendations via Spotify. It also guides the user in creating a logo using Adobe Express, showcasing seamless integration and workflow simplification across applications.

raw_segments: The video opens with a close-up of a woman with blonde hair, wearing a floral top and navy cardigan, speaking in an office setting, her voice heard saying, "time back with the patient." This transitions to a hand typing on a laptop keyboard, followed by a man in a white shirt and tie smiling while interacting with a woman in a striped shirt, as a male voiceover explains how this "allows for the patient and doctor to be a patient and a doctor, like it used to be." A young woman with glasses, wearing a gray patterned shirt, is then shown working on a laptop in a modern office, while another male voiceover emphasizes that it's "fundamentally about amplifying what every person is able to do." An upbeat, inspiring background music begins as a text overlay appears, stating 'To build for tomorrow, and accelerate human progress today,' accompanied by a smiling emoji. The scene shifts to a rural setting where a woman in a blue sari and glasses stands beside a man in traditional white attire, sitting on a stone wall, while a female voiceover highlights how this "opens up the opportunity for that farmer, for that girl in the village who's trying to go to college, to find out what more can they do with their life." The music continues as a woman in a black blazer and pink shirt speaks passionately in a conference room, followed by the reappearance of the text overlay, now reading 'Let’s build that future... together.' A group of people in a collaborative workspace, including a man with a beard and a woman with blonde hair, are then seen working with a small robot, accompanied by the distinct sound of typing. Two women high-five enthusiastically at a table with laptops and drinks, before a text message exchange appears on screen. The video then transitions to a vibrant, abstract 'Microsoft Build' title screen with gradient colors of orange, yellow, and purple, as the background music swells to a grand, orchestral sound. Finally, a man in a black shirt and jeans walks confidently across a stage displaying the 'Microsoft Build' logo in a large conference hall, illuminated with blue and purple lights, greeted by enthusiastic applause from the attentive audience after he exclaims, "Good morning!"

The video opens with a wide shot of a large, enthusiastic audience at a Microsoft Build event, illuminated by vibrant blue and purple stage lighting, many attendees raising their hands or recording the moment, accompanied by the sound of their applause and cheers. The scene transitions to a full-stage view where Satya Nadella, identified by on-screen text as Chairman and CEO of Microsoft, walks confidently across the stage. Dressed casually in a dark navy hoodie over a gray shirt, black pants, and black sneakers with white soles, he greets the audience with an enthusiastic "Good morning, it's fantastic to see you all, welcome to Build!" against a dynamic, colorful abstract backdrop featuring the 'Microsoft Build' logo in bold, modern font with an orange, yellow, and purple gradient. As Nadella speaks, his voice is clear and welcoming, expressing excitement to be back together physically and acknowledging those joining online. He gestures with his hands, occasionally clasping them or using open hand movements to emphasize points, describing developer conferences as "special times, special places to be, especially when platform shifts are in the air." The camera follows him, shifting from wide shots to medium close-ups, capturing his engaged expression and animated delivery as he recalls his first PDC in 1991 and the life-changing impact it had. He conveys an energetic and forward-looking atmosphere, stating it's "exciting to be able to come back to Build 2023 with that same sense of anticipation of something big that is shifting around us as developers," before mentioning reading "Mitchell Waldrop's Dream Machine" last summer. The bright and professional lighting consistently highlights him and the vivid stage design.

Satya Nadella, Chairman and CEO of Microsoft, walks confidently across the vibrantly lit stage at the Microsoft Build event, dressed in a dark navy hoodie, gray shirt, black pants, and black sneakers. As he gestures expressively, the camera pulls back to reveal a large, engaged Conference Hall Audience, many raising their hands or recording, while massive screens display the 'Microsoft Build' branding. Moving to the center, Nadella introduces a new presentation slide titled 'Pursuit of the Dream Machine,' which features a timeline of computing history. He explains, "while I was playing with DB3 as GPT4 was called then, DaVinci 3... it just brought to it brought in perspective what this is all about. I think that that concept of Dream Machine perhaps best communicates what we have really been doing over the last 70 years." As he walks along the stage, pointing to milestones, he elaborates on the evolution, referencing "Vannevar Bush wrote in his most seminal paper, As We May Think," and "Licklider was the first one who even conceptualized the human computer symbiosis," along with "The Mother of All Demos that came in '68," "the Xerox Alto," and "the PC server one in '91, '93." His deliberate movements and hand gestures, particularly when discussing the 2022 ChatGPT milestone, underscore the dynamic and forward-looking atmosphere, keeping the audience's attention fixed on the evolving visual narrative and his insightful commentary.

Satya Nadella, Chairman and CEO of Microsoft, walks confidently across a large, blue-lit stage, dressed in a dark navy hoodie, gray shirt, black pants, and black sneakers. Behind him, a massive screen displays the 'Pursuit of the Dream Machine' timeline, charting computing milestones from 1945 to 2022, including 'As We May Think,' 'The Mother of All Demos,' and ChatGPT. Speaking in a clear, articulate voice, Nadella reflects on past innovations like the "Mosaic moment" and the "iPhone and the cloud," emphasizing that "all of these will be one continuous journey." He references Steve Jobs's description of computers as "bicycles for the mind," calling it a "beautiful metaphor" that captures the "essence of what computing is." As he gestures expressively, the camera pans across a large, engaged Conference Hall Audience, illuminated by blue and purple lights, with some attendees raising hands or recording on smartphones. Nadella continues, explaining that "last November, we got an upgrade," transitioning "from the bicycle to the steam engine" with the "launch of ChatGPT." He describes this as "the Mosaic moment for this generation of the AI platform," looking forward to "what we can do going forward" as developers. He concludes by stating it's an "exciting time" where "every layer of the software stack is going to be changed forever," and there's "no better place to start than the actual..." The dynamic and futuristic atmosphere, reinforced by the multiple screens showing the timeline, underscores the theme of technological evolution.

The video opens with Satya Nadella, Chairman and CEO of Microsoft, standing confidently on a large stage at the Microsoft Build event, dressed in a dark navy hoodie over a gray shirt, black pants, and black sneakers with white soles. He speaks in a clear, articulate voice, gesturing expressively as he discusses the evolving 'developer stack' and how 'we build is fundamentally changing,' with the backdrop displaying 'How we build' in bold white letters. The camera then pulls back to reveal a wide shot of the audience in a dimly lit conference hall, illuminated by blue and purple stage lights, with attendees seated in rows. Satya continues, introducing Microsoft’s developer tools: first, 'Codespaces,' which he highlights for enabling environment setup 'in seconds versus minutes,' as a slide shows a GitHub repository interface. He then moves to 'Dev Box,' noting that it can be set up 'in less than an hour' instead of a day, while a Visual Studio Code-like environment is displayed. Next, he presents 'Copilot,' mentioning 'Copilot X' and its role in driving productivity, which is '54% or so up,' as a screen capture demonstrates AI-assisted code generation. Finally, he discusses 'GitHub Actions and Azure Development environments,' emphasizing the desire to 'stay in VS Code' and 'command line' to do 'everything there,' as a slide illustrates deployment workflows. The scene returns to Satya Nadella on stage, now standing beside a large screen displaying a promotional image for a .NET-powered podcast app, with text 'powered by .NET MAUI & Blazor' and download buttons, as he concludes his point about being 'close, close to that dream.' The stage features modern lighting with vertical blue and teal light strips and 'Microsoft Build' in white lettering, reinforcing the professional, tech-focused, and forward-looking atmosphere of innovation.

The video opens with a dark blue background showcasing four distinct development tools: Codespaces, Microsoft Dev Box, GitHub Copilot, and GitHub Actions and Azure Deployment Environments, each with a screen capture and label. The scene then transitions to Satya Nadella, Chairman and CEO of Microsoft, a bald man with glasses wearing a dark navy hoodie over a gray shirt, black pants, and black sneakers with white soles. He stands on a stage in front of a large screen displaying GitHub Copilot code suggestions, gesturing expressively as he speaks about bringing back "both the joy of programming and the flow of programming," emphasizing that "that ability to be able to stay on task is just so wonderful to see." He continues, stating, "So how we build software is radically different, but what we are going to build as developers is really the story of this developer conference." The camera pulls back to reveal a wide shot of the audience in a dimly lit conference hall, illuminated by blue and purple lights, as Nadella walks confidently across the stage. The screen behind him changes to display the text 'How we build | What we build,' which he gestures toward. He then moves across the stage, pointing to different sections of a dynamic grid of multiple screens showcasing various AI-powered applications and interfaces, including chatbots, code editors, and productivity tools. The grid highlights announcements like 'Microsoft and OpenAI extend partnership' and 'GPT-4 now on Azure OpenAI,' underscoring the integration of AI across Microsoft's ecosystem. Nadella maintains an engaging presence, using hand gestures to emphasize points, while the stage lighting remains focused on him and the vibrant display. He explains that "every week there is something new" and they are "infusing this new AI stack across all layers of it," specifically mentioning starting with "tooling in GitHub" and "Copilot in GitHub," then "Copilot in Power Platform," and "when it comes to productivity, Copilot in Microsoft 365."

The video opens with a close-up of Satya Nadella, Chairman and CEO of Microsoft, a bald man with glasses dressed in a dark navy hoodie over a gray shirt, black pants, and black sneakers with white soles. He stands on a stage, gesturing expressively as his clear, confident voice details the pervasive integration of AI across Microsoft's platforms. Behind him, a massive, dynamic digital wall displays a grid of various Microsoft AI and development tools, including GitHub Copilot, Azure OpenAI Service, and ChatGPT interfaces. Nadella speaks of "Copilot in Viva," "business process," "Copilot in Dynamics 365," "industry workflows," "security Copilot," and "Copilot for the web in Bing and Edge," along with "features in LinkedIn which are driven by AI" and the "AI infrastructure, where Azure Open AI APIs and everything else around it," emphasizing that "every layer of the stack is profoundly changing." As the camera pulls back to reveal the full stage, illuminated with blue and green lighting and featuring the 'Microsoft Build' logo on the floor, Nadella announces, "we're going to have 50 plus more announcements." Simultaneously, the background screens transition to display bold text: '50+ new updates.' He then highlights the first major announcement: "we're bringing search grounding in Bing to ChatGPT," as the screen transitions again to 'Announcing Bringing Bing to ChatGPT.' The audience, seated attentively in the darkened conference hall, erupts in enthusiastic clapping and cheering upon Nadella's invitation, "Yeah, you can clap," underscoring the energetic and futuristic atmosphere surrounding Microsoft’s advancements in AI integration.

The video segment opens with a wide shot of a dimly lit auditorium, bathed in blue stage lighting, filled with an attentive audience. On a modern stage, featuring vertical teal and blue light panels and a sleek wooden floor, Satya Nadella, Chairman and CEO of Microsoft, confidently presents. Dressed in a dark navy hoodie over a gray shirt, black pants, and black sneakers with white soles, he walks with expressive hand gestures, stating that "ChatGPT is the most sort of fast-growing consumer app we've ever seen." Behind him, a large screen displays 'Bringing Bing to ChatGPT' with a screenshot of the ChatGPT interface, showing 'Browsing with Bing' and 'Code Execution Beta.' He explains that "search grounding is a very key feature" for current and grounded information, expressing excitement for its immediate launch in ChatGPT Plus and upcoming free tier availability, calling it "just the start" of bringing "the best of Bing to the ChatGPT experience" with OpenAI. The screen then dramatically transitions to announce 'Windows Copilot' in bold white text under a pink 'Announcing' tag. Satya declares, "Next, we're bringing the Copilot to the biggest canvas of all, Windows," which is met with enthusiastic applause and cheers from the engaged audience, some raising hands or recording. He adds that "you're going to hear a lot from Panos tomorrow about it," and believes this will "make every user a power user of Windows," before instructing, "Let's roll the video," as the segment concludes with the sound of a video starting.

The video begins with a dynamic, abstract blue and purple animated background, accompanied by a futuristic, ambient synth soundscape that builds in intensity. This transitions into the Windows 11 desktop interface, featuring a vibrant blue, flowing ribbon wallpaper, as an upbeat, modern electronic background music with a driving beat begins to play. The taskbar displays the new 'Search' bar and icons, including the Windows Copilot icon, which is clicked to open the Copilot sidebar. The clean, minimalist Windows Copilot interface appears, offering AI-powered assistance. The user types a query, 'How can I adjust my system to get work done?', and Copilot responds with personalized tips, including enabling a focus timer and switching to Dark Theme. The user selects 'Yes' to both suggestions, triggering the activation of the circular Focus Timer widget and the Dark Theme, which changes the desktop to a darker color scheme. The screen then shows multiple open applications, including Visual Studio Code, a dashboard, and system performance widgets, all now in dark mode, emphasizing productivity, while the electronic music continues. The user then asks Copilot to summarize a document titled 'Contoso Business Plan,' and Copilot generates a concise summary. Next, the user asks for music recommendations, and Copilot suggests Spotify playlists like 'Chill Vibes,' 'Brain Food,' and 'Deep Study.' The user selects 'Chill Vibes,' which opens the Spotify Interface, and the background music continues, consistent with music playing. The user then asks how to create a logo for a coffee business, and Copilot suggests using Adobe Express, launching the app and guiding the user to create a logo with a coffee cup design. The final scene shows the completed logo in the Adobe Express Interface, with Copilot confirming the action and offering further assistance, highlighting seamless integration and workflow simplification across apps, all set to the continuous electronic background music.

event_blocks.summary: The demonstration commenced with an AI-powered logo creation for 'THE FOURTH COFFEE' using Adobe Express, followed by its sharing in a Microsoft Teams chat, eliciting positive emoji reactions. The visual narrative then shifted to an abstract background, juxtaposing Spotify and Windows Copilot to signify cross-application integration, and showcased a 'Forza Horizon 5' dashboard merging gaming and productivity. The scene transitioned to a live stage presentation at Microsoft Build, where the 'Windows 11' logo appeared amidst upbeat music and applause. Satya Nadella, clad in a navy hoodie and sneakers, confidently walked onto the stage, gesturing towards a large screen announcing 'Windows Copilot.' He announced the launch of the 'Copilot stack,' explaining its availability for developers to build their own Copilots, detailing its layered architecture from 'AI infrastructure' to 'Foundation models' and 'AI orchestration,' as the attentive audience watched, some recording. Nadella, on stage, elaborated on the common extensibility across platforms like ChatGPT, Bing Chat, and Microsoft 365 Copilot, enabling developers to create plugins reaching billions of users, as a slide titled 'Announcing Copilot extensibility and plugins' was displayed. Yusuf Mehdi, CVP, Consumer CMO, then took the stage, announcing the integration of ChatGPT and Bing as the default search experience for higher quality, timely answers, accompanied by audience applause. A demonstration showed Yusuf selecting 'GPT-4' and enabling 'Browsing with Bing Beta' in ChatGPT to query about .NET team highlights at Build, receiving a detailed response with citations. The interface then switched to 'Plugins' mode, with Mehdi announcing interoperability between ChatGPT and Bing plugins, stating, "So you write them once and they're going to run everywhere." The video concluded with a close-up of the ChatGPT interface showing 'Instacart' and 'Zillow' enabled plugins, and a new Bing chat window with conversation style options. The Microsoft Bing interface was then shown querying Chicago neighborhoods for restaurants, with Bing Chat demonstrating plugin functionality by searching for houses in Hyde Park under $1 million via Zillow, displaying property details and city guides. Mehdi, on stage, explained plugins working across the web via Edge, and a demonstration showed Bing Chat extracting ingredients from a Fourth Coffee recipe webpage to generate a shopping list, highlighting Bing's ability to "read the context of the webpage" and "understand those ingredients," with the shopping list being refined based on user input.

raw_segments: The video opens with a dynamic demonstration of AI-powered collaboration, showing a user interacting with a chat interface to create a logo for 'THE FOURTH COFFEE' using Adobe Express, then sharing it in a Microsoft Teams chat named 'Design Squad' where team members react positively with emojis and messages. The video then shifts to a live stage presentation at a Microsoft event, where the 'Windows 11' logo appears, accompanied by upbeat electronic background music that fades into enthusiastic applause. A voice exclaims, "Oh, it's so cool!" as Satya Nadella, the stage presenter, walks confidently across the stage in a dark navy hoodie, black pants, and sneakers, in front of a large screen announcing 'Windows Copilot.' He gestures expressively as he begins to speak, mentioning they will "talk a lot more about Windows... tomorrow when Panos is up here," before excitedly announcing the launch of "the Copilot stack." He explains that "after all we've built all these Copilots with one common architectural stack, we want to make that available so that everyone here can build their own Copilot for their applications." As he speaks, a layered diagram of the 'Copilot stack' is displayed, showing components like 'Apps,' 'Plugin extensibility,' 'Copilots,' 'AI orchestration,' 'Foundation models,' and 'AI infrastructure,' with arrows indicating bidirectional interaction, and he begins to detail these, starting with "everything from the AI infrastructure to the foundation models, to the..." The audience, seated in rows under blue and purple stage lighting, watches attentively, some raising their hands or recording the moment.

The segment opens with Satya Nadella, Microsoft's Chairman and CEO, on stage at Microsoft Build, actively gesturing in a dark navy hoodie, gray shirt, black pants, and black sneakers. Behind him, a large screen displays a 'Copilot stack' diagram, illustrating components from 'AI orchestration' to 'your Copilots' and their 'extensibility.' Nadella, speaking with a clear, professional tone, emphasizes the common extensibility across platforms like ChatGPT, Bing Chat, and Microsoft 365 Copilot, enabling developers to write plugins that can reach billions of users. The background screen shifts to a vibrant orange, yellow, and purple abstract gradient as Mehdi addresses the audience, discussing the fast progress on delivering the vision of Copilot for the web and business, amidst audience applause.

Yusuf Mehdi, CVP, Consumer CMO at Microsoft, stands on a stage with a vibrant, abstract gradient background of orange, pink, and purple hues, dressed in a black zip-up hoodie and dark pants with a white smartwatch. He gestures expressively, holding a small black device, as he excitedly announces, in a clear voice, that they are bringing "chat GPT and Bing together as with the default search experience. To give you higher quality answers and more timely answers." The scene transitions to a wide shot, revealing a large audience, many recording, while a massive screen behind him displays the OpenAI logo and the Microsoft Bing logo connected by a heart symbol, indicating their partnership. The screen then shifts to a live demonstration of the ChatGPT interface, where Yusuf selects 'GPT-4' and enables 'Browsing with Bing Beta.' He types the query, 'What can I expect from the .NET team at Build this week?' As the AI browses the web, Yusuf explains that "the results now are more up to date. They include fresh content, and they include citations," noting that links can be clicked to go straight to Bing-sourced webpages. The interface generates a detailed response listing six highlights from Microsoft Build 2023, including .NET Multi-platform App UI, cloud-native development, performance, C# 12, and .NET 8. The screen then switches to the 'Plugins' mode, initially showing 'No plugins enabled.' Yusuf returns to the stage, continuing his presentation with animated gestures, stating, "We're also excited to announce that we're going to bring interoperability between ChatGPT and Bing for plugins," which is met with audience applause. He adds, "So you write them once and they're going to run everywhere." The video concludes with a close-up of the ChatGPT interface, now showing the 'Plugins' dropdown with 'Instacart' and 'Zillow' enabled, and a new chat window titled 'Welcome to the new Bing,' featuring a search bar and conversation style options like 'More Creative,' 'More Balanced,' and 'More Precise,' as Yusuf mentions having "Zillow and Instacart enabled."

The segment opens with a close-up of the clean, minimalist Microsoft Bing interface, where a user queries Chicago neighborhoods with good restaurants. Yusuf Mehdi, CVP, Consumer CMO at Microsoft, is heard explaining that Bing Chat now has the same plugins as ChatGPT, demonstrating how he can search for houses in Chicago, ask for criteria, and learn about neighborhoods. Visually, Bing responds with a list including Lincoln Park and Hyde Park, then retrieves real estate listings from Zillow for Hyde Park under $1 million, displaying property details and images, aligning with Yusuf's audio description of automatically calling Zillow for houses in a specific price range and getting "great options" along with "helpful city guides and maps." The scene transitions to Yusuf Mehdi on stage, gesturing animatedly against a vibrant, abstract background, as he continues to elaborate on how plugins will work "across the entire web" via the Edge browser. The screen then displays a Peach Melba Cake recipe from Fourth Coffee, and Yusuf's voice explains being on a webpage, checking out a recipe, and asking Bing Chat to "give me the ingredients from this webpage." Bing is shown generating a shopping list, excluding items the user already has, as Yusuf highlights Bing's ability to "read the context of the webpage" and "understand those ingredients." The segment briefly returns to Yusuf presenting to an engaged audience in a large conference hall, with the recipe on a large screen beside him, before concluding with the Bing interface refining the shopping list based on user input.

event_blocks.summary: The video begins with a Microsoft Bing interface demonstrating an Instacart plugin that automatically adds recipe ingredients to a shopping cart for one-click delivery, highlighting productivity benefits. He then showcases Microsoft 365 Copilot within a Word document titled 'Relecloud Sales Agreement,' explaining its ability to draft and edit legal clauses by calling plugins from Thompson Reuters, specifically demonstrating how it can understand and modify a limitation of liability clause using the Practical Law plugin, check enforceability under California law, and provide related legal resources. The demonstration continues with Copilot in Word, utilizing plugins like Westlaw for legal analysis and summarizing changes in a table. Yusuf Mehdi, identified as CVP, Consumer CMO at Microsoft, is shown on stage with a split-screen view of the Word document and Copilot sidebar. The scene shifts to a Microsoft Teams interface where Copilot, using the Atlassian plugin, responds to a query about 'Contoso's website change management procedures' by listing steps and then facilitates the creation of a Jira ticket for tracking these changes, automatically populating ticket details and allowing assignee selection via Azure Active Directory, with the ticket creation confirmed in the Teams chat. The presentation concludes with Yusuf Mehdi on stage, emphasizing how plugins and Microsoft 365 Copilot enable intelligent reading.

event_blocks.summary: Yusuf Mehdi, CVP, Consumer CMO at Microsoft, stood on stage in a black zip-up hoodie against a vibrant, abstract gradient background, explaining the integration of Microsoft's tools across business apps and the Microsoft Graph for user flow. He enthusiastically announced the Windows Copilot, stating it would change PC usage forever and proceeded to demonstrate its capabilities. The scene shifted to a split-screen showing Yusuf and the 'Windows Copilot' logo, a blue hexagonal icon, against a dynamic gradient. Yusuf, describing a coding project, expressed his intent to configure his PC for creativity and productivity. The view then transitioned to a Windows 11 desktop interface with Visual Studio Code open, where Yusuf narrated invoking Windows Copilot by clicking its taskbar icon. The Windows Copilot sidebar, a minimalist blue and white interface, appeared on the right, consistent across apps. Yusuf posed a query, "How can I adjust my system to get work done?", to which Copilot responded with suggestions including enabling a Focus Session and switching to Dark Theme. Yusuf demonstrated taking action on these suggestions with a single click, which activated the Focus Timer widget, a circular blue and white countdown, and instantly changed the desktop theme to dark mode, including Visual Studio Code and the taskbar. He noted developers' preference for dark mode, remarking on the ease of switching. The Copilot sidebar confirmed the actions, "Started a Focus Session" and "Switched to Dark Theme," as the video concluded with the desktop in dark mode, the Focus Timer active, and Copilot ready for further interaction.

event_blocks.summary: Satya Nadella, dressed in a dark navy hoodie and black pants, confidently walked across the stage at the Microsoft Build event, gesturing expressively while discussing AI opportunities and the "next thing for us, which is really exciting, is AI Studio." A screen announced 'Announcing Azure AI Safety,' highlighting 'Testing,' 'Provenance,' and 'Deployment.' Satya explained that embedding AI safety into the "everyday tool chain" is addressed by "AI Studio," detailing the 'Responsible AI dashboard' for safe development and 'Grounding in prompt flow' under 'Testing,' and 'media provenance' for images/videos and 'watermarking for your neural voice' under 'Provenance,' aligning with screen points on 'Media provenance in Bing and Designer' and 'Audio watermarking in Custom Neural Voice.' event_blocks.summary: On a blue and teal-lit stage, Satya Nadella, wearing a dark navy hoodie and glasses, addressed an audience, gesturing expressively while a large screen behind him announced 'Azure AI Safety,' highlighting features like the Responsible AI dashboard, media provenance in Bing and Designer, and Azure AI Content Safety under Testing, Provenance, and Deployment pillars. Nadella emphasized the critical importance of making AI applications safe at deployment time, stressing continuous monitoring for model drift, and expressed enthusiasm for Azure AI Studio's role in enabling developers to build AI with safety as a priority. The scene then transitioned to a screen capture of the Azure AI Studio interface, introduced by a narrator as a full lifecycle tool for building, customizing, training, evaluating, and responsibly deploying next-generation models, showcasing its clean, modern design with options like 'Create notebook' and 'Add compute,' concluding with a demonstration of the 'Chat playground.' The video further detailed the 'Chat playground' where a user grounded AI models with data from 'Azure Cognitive Search' to create customized conversational experiences, and explored 'Data Management' settings. The assistant successfully responded to a query about hiking jackets. Navigating to the 'Model catalog' in Azure AI Machine Learning Studio, the narrator highlighted the availability of popular models from Azure OpenAI Service, Hugging Face, and other open-source sources. The 'Prompt Flow' interface was then shown, demonstrating how developers can combine organizational data and edit prompts for improved results using foundation, internally developed, or open-source models with tools like Langchain and Semantic Kernel. Finally, the 'Azure AI Content Safety Studio' was presented, allowing users to test and evaluate AI deployments for safety by adjusting severity thresholds for categories like Violence, as exemplified by a rejected sample text related to cutting a path in a forest. The video concluded with a blue screen displaying 'Azure AI Studio' and the tagline 'to build the next generation of AI applications,' reiterated by the narrator.

event_blocks.summary: On a dimly lit stage, Satya Nadella, wearing a dark navy hoodie over a gray shirt, black pants, and black sneakers, presented the concept of 'Data analytics for the era of AI' against a dark blue backdrop highlighting unified storage, experience, governance, and business models. The video introduced 'Microsoft Fabric,' a unified data analytics platform with a teal logo and tagline 'Explore the experience,' showcasing its six core components: Data Factory, Synapse Data Engineering, Synapse Data Warehouse, Synapse Data Science, Synapse Real-Time Analytics, and Power BI. A narrator described Fabric as 'One product, one experience, one architecture, one business model,' then highlighted the 'OneLake data hub' where unified data from Finance, HR, and Sales domains is stored in an open format within a SaaS data lake. This single copy of data is demonstrated as being usable for training machine learning models, visualizing data, and running SQL queries, shown through a data science notebook generating histograms and a SQL query editor displaying revenue data, with a brief glimpse of a 'Sales Team' workspace. The unified experience interface then displayed integration between Power BI, Data Factory, Data Activator, Synapse, Data Science, Data Warehouse, and Real-Time Analytics, with the narrator explaining it consolidates tools for data professionals, including pipelines for data movement and experiments for machine learning model training, visualized by a data pipeline and an MLflow notebook. The summary concluded with a Power BI dashboard featuring customer profile analytics, transitioning to the Power BI home screen, as the narrator began to address business users, emphasizing Fabric's seamless, integrated data analytics environment.

The video opens with a close-up of Satya Nadella, a bald man with glasses, wearing a dark navy hoodie over a gray shirt, black pants, and black sneakers with white soles. He stands on a dimly lit stage, gesturing expressively as he speaks with a clear, articulate voice, emphasizing the need to be on a 'fast pace to build things that help us build this next generation of applications, but build them with safety first.' Behind him, a large screen displays 'Data analytics for the era of AI' and the four pillars of Microsoft Fabric: 'Unified storage and compute,' 'Unified experience,' 'Unified governance,' and 'Unified business model.' The camera pulls back to reveal the modern stage with blue and teal vertical light strips and a large audience. As Nadella continues, the screen transitions to 'What we build' and then 'Why we build,' prompting him to ask the audience, 'one of the things that I think we should ask ourselves as developers, is why do we build? Why do we build technology?' The scene then shifts to a detailed graph titled 'Pursuit of GDP growth,' which Nadella refers to as 'pretty stunning.' This graph, sourced from Maddison Project and OurWorldInData.org, illustrates global GDP on a logarithmic scale from 1000 to today, marking technological revolutions like the printing press, steam engines, and the information revolution. Nadella explains how for 'most of human history,' there wasn't much economic growth or technology, until 'something happened 250 years ago,' referring to the long period from 'the Enlightenment to scientific revolution to the Industrial Revolution,' which he notes was close to 400 years in building. The graph highlights increasing GDP growth multipliers per century, with a projected '+AI' surge, underscoring the professional and forward-looking atmosphere emphasizing AI's transformative economic impact.

The segment opens with a static presentation slide titled 'Pursuit of GDP growth,' displaying a logarithmic graph of global GDP from 1000 to today, marked with key technological revolutions and projected '+AI' surge, alongside GDP growth multipliers. Satya Nadella, dressed in a dark navy hoodie over a gray shirt, black pants, and black sneakers with white soles, walks confidently onto the Microsoft Build stage, gesturing towards the screen as he speaks about 'real progress,' noting the 'slope going upwards' and how information technology has played a role in 'seminal moments' on the march towards that 'dream machine.' He emphasizes that 'we now enter the age of AI and we get to define what that slope looks like going forward for economic growth.' The slide then transitions to a 'Historical index of human development' graph, showing two lines tracking progress from 1870, with a magenta line representing achievement in health, knowledge, and living standards in the United States. Nadella continues, asserting that 'it's not even just economic growth on its own,' but that 'we want economic growth so that we can have human development index growth.' He passionately states, 'We want the lifespans to go up, we want education and prosperity and standard of living to go up everywhere. That's why we build, that's why we innovate, that's why technology exists. It's not for technology's sake, but it is for that broad impact.' As he speaks with expressive hand movements, the camera zooms in before shifting to a dynamic collage of diverse, monochrome images depicting people working and collaborating. This collage then transforms into a vibrant, full-color version, with Nadella still presenting in front of it, the stage illuminated with blue and teal lighting, and the 'Microsoft Build' logo visible on the floor. He then transitions to a personal anecdote, mentioning, 'And to me, this all came together, you know, in January when I was visiting India.'

The video opens with a wide shot of a dimly lit conference hall, where a large audience is seated, facing a stage illuminated by vibrant blue and teal lighting. A massive, dynamic collage of images, depicting diverse people engaged in various activities—from using smartphones and laptops to working in solar fields—serves as a visual backdrop. Satya Nadella, a bald man with glasses, dressed in a dark navy hoodie over a gray shirt, black pants, and black sneakers with white soles, walks confidently across the stage, gesturing expressively as he speaks. His clear, confident voice explains how a demo had a "profound impact" on him, motivating him to approach the "next wave" with rigor to "ensure that this time around, this technology reaches everybody in the world." As he moves from left to right, occasionally pausing to emphasize points with hand movements, the background collage subtly shifts, highlighting scenes like a woman in a blue sari or a man in traditional white attire. Nadella continues, stating that "two things stood out": the ability for what "we build" to "make a difference to 8 billion people, not some small sort of group of people." He stresses the importance of achieving this through "diffusion that takes days and weeks, not years and centuries," driven by the desire for "equitable growth" and "trust in technology." The engaged audience, some raising their hands or recording, contributes to the energetic atmosphere of this inclusive tech presentation.

event_blocks.summary: Satya Nadella, wearing a dark navy hoodie, presented on a modern stage about protecting fundamental rights, managing energy transition, and empowering global citizens, with a dynamic screen behind him displaying diverse scenes and transitioning to a split-screen of rural Indian farmers and a smartphone user, concluding with the Microsoft Build logo and applause. The narrative then shifted to a video featuring Mohd Abduallah Khan, a farmer in a rural Indian village, who explained in Hindi his farming practices and the lack of information access in his region, where villages are limited to newspapers and word-of-mouth, as illustrated by a man operating a manual press machine under a yellow canopy. Further in the video, a woman in a yellow and red sari was seen at a village shop, while text overlays highlighted the unawareness of government schemes. A young man in a white kurta and cap spoke about these schemes not reaching rural areas, leading to a transition to an office setting where Aaditeshwar Seth, Co-Founder of Gram Vaani and Professor at IIT Delhi, discussed 'media dark areas' and literacy challenges. The scene returned to the village with an elderly man, a group of women and children, and children in school uniforms entering a classroom, before shifting back to the office where Smita Gupta, Lawyer and Technologist at OpenNyAI, addressed the challenge of providing justice in India's numerous languages. Abhigyan Raman, Senior AI Researcher at AI4Bharat, was shown discussing with colleagues, including a woman wearing headphones verifying a recording on a tablet, and then explaining the 'Jugalbandi' chatbot system architecture on a digital whiteboard, detailing its components like 'Govt: Scheme Details,' 'Chat layer (WhatsApp),' 'AI4Bharat Models,' and 'Azure Machine Learning,' emphasizing its role in solving linguistic and technological divides with extensive data for machine translation and speech recognition, utilizing models like Parshini and Azure, and concluding with a passionate emphasis on AI's technical and social integration in rural development. The video continued with a man in glasses and a dark polo shirt explaining AI's language capabilities, visually supported by Python code and AI4Bharat metrics, followed by a scene in a rural Indian village where a woman in a blue sari assisted an elderly man, and a younger man demonstrated how to use a smartphone messaging app to access government scheme information via text and voice. The elderly man inquired about schemes, identifying as a farmer, and a female AI voice, 'Jugalbandi,' responded in Hindi offering assistance. The elderly and younger men were then seen discussing the AI's detailed response, including required documents and forms in text and audio. The narrative concluded in a modern office with a woman in a black blazer explaining the Ministry of Electronics and IT's five-year effort and ChatGPT's catalytic role, ending with a view of a Microsoft Azure Machine Learning Studio dashboard displaying AI endpoint performance metrics.

raw_segments: The minute opens with Satya Nadella, a bald man with glasses in a dark navy hoodie, presenting on a modern stage. He speaks with expressive gestures about protecting fundamental rights, managing energy transition with finite resources, and empowering every person and organization on the planet. Behind him, a large dynamic screen displays a collage of diverse scenes, including a woman on a laptop and a person in a safety vest, which then transitions to a split-screen showing two men in traditional white attire in a rural Indian field on the left and a close-up of a hand typing on a smartphone on the right. The camera zooms out to reveal the Microsoft Build logo on the floor as Nadella concludes his speech, emphasizing the profound impact developers will have on 8 billion people, and introduces a video. Applause erupts, followed by upbeat music. The scene shifts to the introduced video segment, featuring Mohd Abduallah Khan, a farmer in traditional white attire, standing in front of a brick house in a rural Indian village. He introduces himself in Hindi, stating he is a farmer in his village with two acres of land, where he grows both rabi and kharif crops like wheat, jowar, millet, sesame, and mustard. The video then cuts to a man operating a manual press machine under a yellow canopy, with a text overlay explaining that in Mewat, many villages lack access to information and are limited to newspapers and word-of-mouth.

The video opens in a rural Indian village, showcasing a small shop with hanging snacks and a woman in a yellow and red sari interacting with a vendor, while text overlays state, 'There are many government schemes available to us, but we often don't know about them.' A young man in a white kurta and cap, standing in a dusty village courtyard, speaks directly to the camera in Hindi, explaining that while government schemes exist, they often don't reach people in rural areas. A wide shot reveals the village landscape with a mosque and distant hills before transitioning to a modern office setting. Here, Aaditeshwar Seth, identified as Co-Founder of Gram Vaani and Professor at IIT Delhi, speaks about communities living in 'media dark areas' where traditional media penetration is sparse and literacy challenges limit access to newspapers. The narrative shifts back to the village, showing an elderly man walking down a narrow lane, followed by a group of women and children gathered near a brick wall. Children in school uniforms walk through a village doorway, leading to a classroom where students in colorful headscarves read textbooks. The scene returns to the office, where Smita Gupta, a Lawyer and Technologist at OpenNyAI, speaks in front of a whiteboard, highlighting India's 22 constitutionally recognized languages and over 120 other languages, emphasizing the challenge and opportunity of providing justice in one's own language. A close-up shows code on a screen, followed by Abhigyan Raman, Senior AI Researcher at AI4Bharat, discussing with colleagues. A woman wearing headphones verifies a recording on a tablet with Hindi text displayed on the screen. Abhigyan Raman then explains a system architecture on a digital whiteboard, illustrating 'Govt: Scheme Details,' 'Chat layer (WhatsApp),' 'AI4Bharat Models,' and 'Azure Machine Learning,' while interacting with a colleague. He passionately describes Jugalbandi as a chatbot built to solve linguistic and technological divides, mentioning the collection of millions of parallel sentences for machine translation and thousands of hours of data for speech recognition, and the use of state-of-the-art models like Parshini speech recognition and Azure. The segment ends with a close-up of Abhigyan Raman speaking passionately, emphasizing the technical and social integration of AI in rural development.

The video opens with a man in glasses and a dark polo shirt speaking about AI capabilities, explaining how they allow understanding, transcribing, translating, and synthesizing language back into speech. This is visually reinforced by a computer screen displaying Python code for an AI inference function and translation metrics from AI4Bharat. The scene then transitions to a rural Indian village, where a woman in a blue sari assists an elderly man in traditional white attire. A younger man in similar white attire explains in Hindi how they can use a smartphone, which displays a messaging app with English and Hindi text, to get information about government schemes by both writing and speaking. The elderly man is then heard asking in Hindi about government schemes, identifying himself as a farmer. A female AI voice, introducing herself as 'Jugalbandi,' responds in Hindi, offering assistance with government schemes and enrollment. The elderly man and the younger man are then seen walking through a dry field, discussing the information, with the younger man detailing that the AI's response included required documents and forms in both text and audio. The narrative shifts to a modern office where a woman in a black blazer and glasses speaks to two men, explaining that the Ministry of Electronics and IT has been working on this for five years, and ChatGPT's launch was a catalyst. The segment concludes with a shot of a Microsoft Azure Machine Learning Studio dashboard, showcasing performance metrics for an AI endpoint, highlighting the technical infrastructure.

event_blocks.summary: The video opens with a tranquil rural landscape at dawn or dusk, featuring a man in traditional white attire traversing a plowed field under a hazy sky, while a group of people, including children in vibrant attire, proceed along a dusty dirt path bordered by trees, all underscored by subtle, uplifting instrumental music. The scene then transitions to a modest classroom where a woman in a pink sari, white shawl, and glasses stands before a blackboard, addressing students who actively raise their hands. A confident female voice with a slight accent narrates, 'We've been able to categorize the diversity of India as an innovation opportunity. And if you're able to do that successfully, you're able to do it for the world.' Subsequently, the setting shifts to a modern office, where a woman in a black blazer over a pink shirt, wearing glasses, confidently gestures while speaking. The segment culminates with the display of the Microsoft logo on a black background.
